channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Flegler penalised for high tackle on Tupouniua, gifting Bulldogs two points
item.description: During a recent NRL clash, Tom Flegler delivered a dangerous high tackle on Jayden Tupouniua, resulting in a penalty that awarded the Bulldogs two points. The incident sparked reactions from fans and ...

item.title: South Africans Walk for Unity and Social Change
item.description: In a country grappling with inequality, unemployment, and social fragmentation, four men – Mshengu Tshabalala, Moses Aphane, Jeremiah Mkhwanazi, and Frankie Motsepe – are undertaking a long-distance j...

item.title: Kerala Congress MLAs Authorise Party High Command to Choose Chief Minister
item.description: In Kerala, the 63-member Congress Legislature Party (CLP) on Thursday formally authorised the party high command to select the nominee for the Chief Minister’s post. The resolution, moved by Kerala Pr...

item.title: Tolashe distances herself from daughter amid household employee salary controversy
item.description: Embattled Social Development Minister Sisisi Tolashe has come under renewed scrutiny after appearing before Parliament’s oversight committee on social development, where she addressed a series of alle...

item.title: Six-Year-Old Fan Draws 100 Animals to Celebrate Sir David Attenborough's 100th Birthday
item.description: Max Evans-Browning, a six-year-old from Penylan, Pembrokeshire, has celebrated Sir David Attenborough's 100th birthday in a remarkable way. As a devoted fan of the renowned broadcaster, Max drew 100 a...

item.title: ODM Leaders in Ruto's Cabinet Eye Return to Party Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Suba South MP Caroli Omondi has claimed that some Cabinet Secretaries who were brought into President William Ruto's broad-based government from the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) are planning to re...

item.title: Cambridge Reschedules AS-Level Mathematics Exam After Paper Leak
item.description: Cambridge International Education (CIE) has decided to cancel the AS-level mathematics exam after it was found that the paper was shared prematurely, affecting regions including Africa, Europe, the Mi...

item.title: Changing Political Landscape in Farrer: Voters Demand Attention and Local Representation
item.description: In Australia’s regional Farrer electorate, a significant political shift is underway following the retirement of long-serving MP Sussan Ley. Traditionally a safe Coalition seat, the by-election has sp...

item.title: Kenya and France to Host Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi, 4,000 Delegates Expected
item.description: President William Ruto of Kenya and President Emmanuel Macron of France will jointly host the Africa Forward Summit 2026 in Nairobi from 10 to 12 May. The summit is expected to gather around 4,000 del...

item.title: India urges Bangladesh to expedite nationality verification for smoother repatriation of illegal immigrants
item.description: Following the BJP’s victory in the West Bengal assembly elections, the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) of India responded to remarks made by Bangladesh regarding alleged 'pushback' incidents. MEA s...

item.title: NAHCON Airlifts 6,635 Nigerian Pilgrims to Saudi Arabia in 14 Flights for 2026 Hajj
item.description: My people, according to the latest update from National Hajj Commission of Nigeria (NAHCON), dem don already carry 6,635 Nigerian pilgrims go Saudi Arabia through 14 different flights as the 2026 Hajj...

item.title: Labour Faces Potential Losses in Local and Devolved Elections: Hourly Overview
item.description: Keir Starmer is facing a critical three-day period as polls open across English councils and the Scottish and Welsh devolved assemblies. Labour is widely expected to suffer significant losses, with fo...

item.title: Senator Gatchalian Pushes for Higher Income Tax Exemption to Boost Workers' Take-Home Pay
item.description: Senator Sherwin Gatchalian is calling for the immediate approval of Senate Bill No. 56, also known as the Granting Increase in Take-Home Pay for All Working Filipinos Act or the Ginhawa bill. The prop...

item.title: How Reading Mediums Affect Comprehension and Learning
item.description: Recent discussions around reading on paper versus digital screens have gained attention after the Swedish government decided to return to physical books in classrooms, citing concerns over declining t...

item.title: Transparency and Choice Needed for Surveillance-Based Pricing
item.description: The Globe and Mail editorial addresses the emerging practice of surveillance pricing, where businesses use personal consumer data to charge different prices to different customers for the same product...

item.title: Study Shows Human Brain Can Process Language During Unconsciousness
item.description: A recent study from Baylor College of Medicine has demonstrated that the human brain is capable of processing complex language even when a person is unconscious under general anaesthesia. Researchers ...

item.title: IFISA and IFC join forces to fund critical water and sanitation projects in South Africa
item.description: South Africa is intensifying efforts to tackle its escalating water crisis through a strategic partnership between the Infrastructure Finance and Implementation Support Agency (IFISA) and the Internat...

item.title: GCash supports ASEAN 2026 Summit with focus on cashless payments and MSME digital inclusion
item.description: GCash, one of the Philippines’ leading fintech platforms, has been announced as an official partner of the 48th ASEAN Summit held in Lapu-Lapu City, Cebu. The partnership aims to highlight cashless tr...

item.title: How Tanzania Secured Syndicated Loans for SGR Expansion
item.description: The Government of Tanzania recently managed to secure a big loan to continue the construction of the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) from Makutopora to Isaka. Standard Chartered Bank announced that it ar...

item.title: Cambridge to Conduct Replacement Maths Exam on June 9 After Paper Leak
item.description: Cambridge International Education (CIE) has announced that it will conduct a replacement AS-level Mathematics exam on June 9 for regions affected by the premature sharing of the original paper. The af...
